"'''Doctor Death: The Tale of Dr. Evazan and Ponda Baba'''" is a short story about [[Cornelius Evazan/Legends|Cornelius Evazan]] and [[Ponda Baba/Legends|Ponda Baba]]. [[Kenneth C. Flint]] wrote the story for ''[[Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina]]'', which [[Bantam Spectra]] published in [[August]] [[1995]].

==Plot summary==
On stormy [[Ando/Legends|Ando]], the mad [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Cornelius Evazan/Legends|Evazan]] meets with his patron, an ambitious [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] [[Senator/Legends|Senator]]. The [[Politics/Legends|politician]] is funding Evazan's research project to develop a medical breakthrough profitable enough to afford Ando independence from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]]. Evazan's project: transference of consciousness as a means of practical [[Immortality/Legends|immortality]]. [[Gurion Silizzar]], a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] hoping to avenge the gruesome [[Death/Legends|deaths]] of his family members at the hands of Evazan, infiltrates the [[Evazan's castle|doctor's gloomy castle]]. The [[Assassin/Legends|assassin's]] attempt is thwarted by [[Rover]], Evazan's pet [[Meduza]], a voracious bloblike creature.
